name: ICICI Sapphiro Credit Card

fees.waiverCondition: Annual fee waived on spending ₹6,00,000 in the previous year

categoryRates.description: 4 PAYBACK Points per ₹100 spent on travel bookings
4 PAYBACK Points per ₹100 spent on dining
2X PAYBACK Points on all weekend (Saturday & Sunday) transactions
2 PAYBACK Points per ₹100 spent on all other retail purchases

welcomeBenefits.condition: On payment of joining fee and card activation

milestoneBenefits.benefit: Bonus PAYBACK Points and annual fee waiver

loungeAccess.provider: Priority Pass / Dreamfolks / Visa Signature Lounges

additionalBenefits: 2X rewards on weekend spends across all categories
Culinary Treats dining program - Buy 1 Get 1 at 1500+ restaurants
Complimentary golf games at select courses
pros: 12 domestic + 4 international lounge visits per year
2X rewards on weekends gives excellent value for weekend shoppers
Lower forex markup at 2% compared to most cards
₹2 Cr air accident cover and ₹50L travel insurance
cons: ₹6,500 annual fee is steep and requires ₹6L spend to waive
Base reward rate still only 0.50% on weekdays
PAYBACK Point value is modest at ₹0.25 per point
Limited international lounge visits compared to Emeralde
idealFor: High spenders who want weekend bonus rewards and travel perks
Frequent travellers who need lounge access and travel insurance
Users who dine out regularly and can leverage Culinary Treats

faqs.question: How does the 2X weekend reward work?
faqs.answer: All transactions made on Saturday and Sunday earn double PAYBACK Points (4 points per ₹100 instead of the usual 2). This applies to all retail categories and is a great way to maximize rewards on larger weekend purchases.

faqs.question: What is included in the travel insurance?
faqs.answer: The card provides up to ₹50 lakh overseas medical insurance covering emergency hospitalization, evacuation, and related expenses. Air accident cover is up to ₹2 crore. Coverage is activated when travel tickets are purchased using the card.

faqs.question: How does the Sapphiro compare to the Emeralde?
faqs.answer: The Emeralde is a tier above with unlimited lounge access, Taj Epicure membership, 1.5% forex markup, and higher reward rates. However, the Emeralde is invite-only with a ₹12,000 fee, while the Sapphiro is more accessible at ₹6,500.

faqs.question: Is the 2% forex markup competitive?
faqs.answer: Yes, 2% is competitive among Indian credit cards. Most cards charge 3.5%, and only a few super-premium cards offer lower rates. For international spends, this card saves 1.5% per transaction compared to standard cards.
